title: Overview - Ontology - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: An operational layer that sits on top of digital assets (datasets, virtual tables, models) and connects them to their real-world counterparts, serving as a digital twin of an organization.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: A schema definition of a real-world entity or event in the Ontology, representing the semantic element that maps datasources into meaningful business concepts.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: A characteristic of an object type that defines metadata fields with rich metadata, security, and governance.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: A schema definition of a relationship between two object types in the Ontology.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: A kinetic element of the Ontology that captures data from operators or orchestrates decision-making processes connecting to existing systems.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: A kinetic element providing a way to author and evolve business logic with arbitrary complexity within the Ontology.

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology type that describes the shape and capabilities of an object type, enabling polymorphism for consistent modeling across object types with a common shape.

extractedEntities.name: Object View
extractedEntities.description: A reusable view configuration for exploring and displaying objects in user-facing applications.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's core platform that hosts the Ontology layer, integrating datasets, virtual tables, and models into a unified semantic and kinetic framework.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Manager
extractedEntities.description: The tool within Foundry used to define and configure ontological elements such as object types, link types, and action types.

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's application builder for creating high-quality user-facing operational applications powered by the Ontology.

extractedEntities.name: Object Explorer
extractedEntities.description: A Foundry tool that allows users to search for and explore objects of interest in the Ontology.

extractedEntities.name: Quiver
extractedEntities.description: A Foundry tool for performing complex analyses on Ontology data.

extractedEntities.name: Digital Twin
extractedEntities.description: An architectural pattern where the Ontology serves as a comprehensive digital representation of the organization, containing both semantic and kinetic elements.

keyInsights: The Palantir Ontology is divided into semantic elements (objects, properties, links) and kinetic elements (actions, functions, dynamic security).
The Ontology sits on top of datasets, virtual tables, and models, mapping raw data to meaningful business concepts rather than exposing tables and columns directly.
Interfaces provide object type polymorphism, allowing consistent modeling across object types that share a common shape.
The Ontology is deeply integrated into Palantir's user-facing tools including Workshop, Object Explorer, and Quiver.
Functions provide a way to author business logic of arbitrary complexity within the Ontology, complementing the more structured action types.
The Ontology serves as a digital twin of the organization, connecting digital assets to their real-world counterparts such as plants, equipment, products, customer orders, and financial transactions.

title: Core concepts - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: The schema definition of a real-world entity or event, analogous to a dataset in the data model.

extractedEntities.name: Object
extractedEntities.description: A single instance of an object type, corresponding to one real-world entity or event, analogous to a row in a dataset.

extractedEntities.name: Object Set
extractedEntities.description: A collection of multiple object instances representing a group of real-world entities or events.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: The schema definition of a characteristic of a real-world entity or event, analogous to a column in a dataset.

extractedEntities.name: Property Value
extractedEntities.description: The value of a property on an object instance, analogous to a field (cell) in a dataset.

extractedEntities.name: Shared Property
extractedEntities.description: A property that can be used across multiple object types, enabling consistent data modeling and centralized metadata management.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: The schema definition of a relationship between two object types, analogous to a join between two datasets.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: The schema definition of a set of changes or edits to objects, property values, and links that a user can take at once, including side effect behaviors.

extractedEntities.name: Role
extractedEntities.description: The central permissioning model in the Ontology, similar to Foundry filesystem roles, granting access to ontological resources.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform where the Ontology provides a rich semantic layer on top of integrated datasets and models.

extractedEntities.name: Dataset-Ontology Mapping
extractedEntities.description: A design pattern where Ontology concepts map directly to dataset structures: object type to dataset, object to row, property to column, property value to field, link type to join.

keyInsights: An object type maps to a dataset, an object to a row, a property to a column, a property value to a field, and a link type to a join between datasets.
Shared properties enable consistent data modeling across multiple object types with centralized metadata management.
An object set is a collection of multiple object instances, representing a filtered or grouped subset of real-world entities.
Action types define both the edits to Ontology data and the side effect behaviors triggered upon submission.
Roles serve as the central permissioning model in the Ontology, granting access to ontological resources similarly to filesystem roles in Foundry.
The Ontology creates a complete picture by mapping both datasets and models to object types, properties, link types, and action types.

title: Object and link types - Object types - Overview - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: The schema definition of a real-world entity or event that maps to backing datasources to create concrete objects in the Ontology.

extractedEntities.name: Object
extractedEntities.description: A single instance of an object type corresponding to one real-world entity or event, such as an individual employee or a specific flight.

extractedEntities.name: Object Set
extractedEntities.description: A collection of multiple object instances, analogous to a filtered set of rows in a dataset, such as 'All tenured employees'.

extractedEntities.name: Backing Datasource
extractedEntities.description: The underlying data source (dataset, virtual table) that provides the actual data values for objects of a given object type.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: A characteristic of an object type that defines what data fields are available on objects of that type.

extractedEntities.name: Shared Property
extractedEntities.description: A property definition that can be reused across multiple object types for consistent data modeling.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Manager
extractedEntities.description: The Foundry tool used to create and configure object types, add backing datasources, and manage the Ontology schema.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform that maps Ontology concepts to actual organizational data, enabling data assets to power real-world applications.

extractedEntities.name: Employee Directory
extractedEntities.description: An example use case where an Employee object type is backed by enterprise employee directory data, with objects representing individual employees like 'Melissa Chang'.

extractedEntities.name: Flight Management
extractedEntities.description: An example use case where a Flight object type represents individual flights with departure and arrival information.

keyInsights: Objects are created by adding backing datasources to an object type in the Ontology Manager, connecting enterprise data into the Ontology.
An object type is analogous to a dataset, an object to a row, and an object set to a filtered set of rows.
The Foundry Ontology is not an abstract data model; it maps each ontological concept to actual organizational data to power real-world applications.
Object sets represent groups of real-world entities, such as 'All tenured employees' or 'All arrived flights'.
Organizations add backing datasources (like employee directories or enterprise data systems) to connect real data into the Ontology.

title: Object and link types - Properties - Overview - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: The schema definition of a characteristic of a real-world entity or event, analogous to a column in a dataset, with associated metadata including base type and visibility settings.

extractedEntities.name: Property Value
extractedEntities.description: The actual data value of a property on an object instance, analogous to a field (cell) in a dataset.

extractedEntities.name: Base Type
extractedEntities.description: The fundamental data type of a property, including String, Integer, Short, Date, Timestamp, Boolean, Byte, Long, Float, Double, Decimal, Vector, Array, Struct, Media Reference, Time Series, and Attachment.

extractedEntities.name: Primary Key
extractedEntities.description: A unique identifier property for an object type; valid key types include String, Integer, Short, and certain others, while Float/Double/Decimal and complex types are excluded.

extractedEntities.name: Title Key
extractedEntities.description: The property used as the display name for an object instance; most base types are valid except Vector, Struct, Media Reference, Time Series, and Attachment.

extractedEntities.name: Render Hints
extractedEntities.description: Metadata that informs how a property value should be displayed in user-facing applications.

extractedEntities.name: Struct
extractedEntities.description: A complex property type that allows structured data within a single property, though it does not support nesting and its fields cannot be arrays.

extractedEntities.name: Shared Property
extractedEntities.description: A reusable property definition that can be applied to multiple object types, enabling centralized management of property metadata.

extractedEntities.name: Visibility Setting
extractedEntities.description: A property-level configuration controlling whether a property is visible or hidden in different application contexts.

extractedEntities.name: Array Property
extractedEntities.description: A property type that holds ordered collections of values; cannot contain null elements, and nested arrays are not supported in Object Storage V2.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: The parent schema that contains properties defining the characteristics of its instances.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Manager
extractedEntities.description: The Foundry tool where properties are defined, configured, and mapped to backing datasources.

keyInsights: Properties have a base type system including commonly used types (String, Integer, Short), time-based types (Date, Timestamp), float-like types (Float, Double, Decimal), and complex types (Array, Struct, Vector).
Not all base types are valid as primary keys; Float, Double, Decimal, Vector, Struct, Media Reference, Time Series, and Attachment cannot serve as primary keys.
Array properties cannot contain null elements, and nested arrays are not supported in Object Storage V2.
Struct properties do not support nesting, and their fields cannot be arrays.
Long values have representational issues in JavaScript for values greater than 1e15, so String is recommended instead.
Boolean properties limit an object type to only two object instances when used as a primary key.
Property values are created by adding backing datasources to an object type in the Ontology Manager, mapping enterprise data columns to Ontology properties.

title: Object and link types - Types reference - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: A schema definition of a real-world entity or event comprised of individual objects; metadata includes display name, property names, property data types, and description.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: A characteristic that informs a real-world entity or event, defined with name and data type within an object type.

extractedEntities.name: Shared Property
extractedEntities.description: A reusable property that can be applied to multiple object types for consistent modeling and centralized metadata management.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: The schema definition of a relationship between two object types, with each instance representing a specific relationship between two objects.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: The schema definition of a set of changes to objects, property values, and links including side effect behaviors triggered on submission.

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology type that describes the shape and capabilities of an object type, enabling polymorphism across types with a common shape.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type Group
extractedEntities.description: A classification primitive that helps users better search and explore their Ontology by grouping related object types.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type Definition
extractedEntities.description: Type-level metadata about ontology entities including display name, property names, property data types, and descriptions, distinct from actual data values.

extractedEntities.name: Object Instance
extractedEntities.description: The actual primary key and property values for a specific instance of an ontology entity, representing concrete data rather than schema metadata.

extractedEntities.name: Data Type
extractedEntities.description: Types used to represent data values in Foundry, inspired by concepts from RDF, OWL, and XSD semantic web standards.

extractedEntities.name: RDF-Inspired Type System
extractedEntities.description: Foundry's data type system is inspired by semantic web standards including RDF, OWL, and XSD for representing ontological data values.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: The platform that categorizes types into Ontology types (for modeling domains) and data types (for representing values).

keyInsights: Foundry distinguishes between Ontology types (used to model real-world domains) and data types (used to represent data values).
Foundry's data type system is inspired by semantic web standards including RDF, OWL, and XSD.
There is a clear distinction between object type definitions (schema metadata) and object instances (actual data values with primary keys and properties).
Object type groups are a classification primitive for organizing and exploring the Ontology, separate from interfaces which define type shapes.
Object type metadata includes display name, property names, property data types, and descriptions, but not actual data values.
Interfaces provide polymorphism for consistent modeling across object types sharing a common shape, distinct from object type groups which are organizational.

title: Object and link types - Link types - Overview - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: The schema definition of a relationship between two object types; links can exist between different types or between objects of the same type.

extractedEntities.name: Link
extractedEntities.description: A single instance of a link type representing a specific relationship between two objects in the same Ontology.

extractedEntities.name: Cardinality
extractedEntities.description: The constraint on how many objects can participate on each side of a link type: one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-one, or many-to-many.

extractedEntities.name: Foreign Key Link
extractedEntities.description: A link implementation for one-to-one and many-to-one cardinalities where the relationship is defined by a foreign key property on one of the object types.

extractedEntities.name: Many-to-Many Link Datasource
extractedEntities.description: A separate backing datasource containing the combinations of linked objects, required for many-to-many cardinality link types.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: The entity type that participates in link relationships; each side of a link type references an object type.

extractedEntities.name: Backing Datasource
extractedEntities.description: The underlying data that provides values for link relationships, either through foreign keys on object types or separate datasources for many-to-many links.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Manager
extractedEntities.description: The tool where link types are created and configured between object types.

extractedEntities.name: Employee-Employer Relationship
extractedEntities.description: An example link type between Employee and Company object types, representing the employment relationship.

extractedEntities.name: Flight-Aircraft Assignment
extractedEntities.description: An example link type between Flight and Aircraft object types, representing the assignment of aircraft to scheduled flights.

extractedEntities.name: Self-Referential Link
extractedEntities.description: A usage pattern where a link type connects an object type to itself, such as a Direct Report to Manager relationship within an Employee type.

keyInsights: Link types support four cardinalities: one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-one, and many-to-many.
One-to-one and many-to-one links are implemented via foreign key properties on the object type, while many-to-many links require a separate backing datasource.
Links can exist between objects of the same type (self-referential), such as a Direct Report to Manager link on an Employee object type.
Links between object types across different Ontologies are not supported; a shared Ontology must be used instead.
Each side of a link type has its own display name, allowing different perspectives on the same relationship.
Link types are analogous to joins between datasets in the relational data model.

title: Action types - Overview - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: The definition of a set of changes or edits to objects, property values, and links that a user can execute as a single atomic transaction.

extractedEntities.name: Action
extractedEntities.description: A single transaction that changes the properties of one or more objects based on user-defined logic, committed atomically to the Ontology.

extractedEntities.name: Rules
extractedEntities.description: The logic component of an action type that defines what Ontology edits occur when the action is submitted.

extractedEntities.name: Parameters
extractedEntities.description: User-input definitions on an action type that collect values needed for the action's rules and logic.

extractedEntities.name: Submission Criteria
extractedEntities.description: Validation conditions that determine whether an action can be submitted, encoding business logic into data editing permissions.

extractedEntities.name: Side Effects
extractedEntities.description: Additional behaviors triggered upon action submission, such as notifications and webhooks.

extractedEntities.name: Writeback Dataset
extractedEntities.description: The dataset that captures the most up-to-date version of object data with all user edits incorporated from actions.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: The entity type that actions operate on, modifying its objects, properties, and links.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: A characteristic of an object type whose values can be modified through action rules.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: A relationship type whose instances can be created or removed through action rules.

extractedEntities.name: Atomic Action Transaction
extractedEntities.description: A design pattern where all changes defined by an action are committed atomically to the Ontology and reflected across all applications simultaneously.

extractedEntities.name: Employee Role Assignment
extractedEntities.description: An example action type (Assign Employee) that changes an employee's role, auto-creates manager links, sends notifications, and validates HR authorization.

keyInsights: An action type consists of four main components: rules (Ontology edits), parameters (user inputs), submission criteria (validations), and side effects (notifications, webhooks).
Actions are committed atomically to the Ontology, meaning all changes in a single action are applied together and reflected across all applications.
The same action logic and validations are available across all user-facing applications, ensuring consistent edits to the Ontology.
Action types enable users to handle data while thinking about overall objectives rather than specific property edits.
Changes from actions are captured in a writeback dataset that contains the most up-to-date version of object data.
Actions allow organizations to encode business rules, notifications, and authorization checks into a single reusable mutation definition.

title: Action types - Rules - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Rules
extractedEntities.description: The logic component of an action type that transforms parameters into Ontology edits or other effects, divided into Ontology rules and effect rules.

extractedEntities.name: Create Object Rule
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology rule that creates a new object of a predefined type, requiring the primary key property to be filled.

extractedEntities.name: Modify Object Rule
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology rule that modifies an existing object whose primary key is derived from object reference parameters.

extractedEntities.name: Create or Modify Object Rule
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology rule that modifies an existing object or creates a new one if none is selected, with either auto-generated or user-submitted primary key.

extractedEntities.name: Delete Object Rule
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology rule that deletes an existing object identified via object reference parameters.

extractedEntities.name: Create Link Rule
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology rule that creates many-to-many links between objects passed via object reference parameters.

extractedEntities.name: Delete Link Rule
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology rule that deletes many-to-many links between objects passed via object reference parameters.

extractedEntities.name: Function Rule
extractedEntities.description: An action rule that references an Ontology edit function whose inputs derive from action parameters; when present, no other rules may be configured.

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: An Ontology type enabling polymorphic actions: create, modify, or delete objects of any type implementing a particular interface.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: The parent entity that contains rules defining the transformation logic from parameters to Ontology edits.

extractedEntities.name: Function-Backed Action
extractedEntities.description: A pattern where a function rule replaces all other rules, with function code handling all Ontology edits, providing maximum flexibility.

extractedEntities.name: Interface-Polymorphic Action
extractedEntities.description: A pattern where action rules operate on interfaces rather than concrete object types, enabling polymorphic create, modify, and delete operations.

keyInsights: Action rules come in two main categories: Ontology rules (create, modify, delete objects and links) and function rules (delegating to Ontology edit functions).
When a function rule is present on an action type, no other rules may be configured because function code handles everything the other rules can do.
Foreign key links (one-to-one, one-to-many) cannot be created or deleted with link rules; instead, the Modify Object rule must update the foreign key property directly.
Actions can operate on interfaces rather than concrete object types, enabling polymorphic create, modify, and delete operations across all implementing types.
Rule values can be sourced from parameters, object parameter properties, conditional logic, or static values.
Create or Modify Object rule supports auto-generated unique IDs as primary keys when no existing object is selected.

title: Action types - Submission criteria - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Submission Criteria
extractedEntities.description: Conditions that determine whether an action can be submitted, supporting business logic encoding into data editing permissions for Ontology data quality and governance.

extractedEntities.name: Condition
extractedEntities.description: A single comparison check between two values that either passes or fails based on parameter or user inputs.

extractedEntities.name: Current User Condition
extractedEntities.description: A condition template that defines permissions based on the submitting user's ID, group memberships, or Multipass attributes.

extractedEntities.name: Parameter Condition
extractedEntities.description: A condition template that checks values of action parameters, such as object properties, against expected values.

extractedEntities.name: Operator
extractedEntities.description: Logical operators (AND, OR, NOT) used to combine and nest conditions into sophisticated logical statements.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: The parent entity containing submission criteria that independently gate whether the action can be submitted.

extractedEntities.name: Group Membership Validation
extractedEntities.description: A security mechanism that checks whether the submitting user belongs to specific groups (direct or inherited) before allowing action submission.

extractedEntities.name: User ID Validation
extractedEntities.description: A security mechanism that checks the submitting user's identity against a statically defined list or a string parameter storing user IDs.

extractedEntities.name: Business Logic Validation
extractedEntities.description: A pattern where business rules are encoded as submission criteria, combining user context, parameter values, and object state to control action submission.

extractedEntities.name: Flight Aircraft Change Control
extractedEntities.description: An example where submission criteria ensure only flight controllers can change aircraft assignments and only operational aircraft can be selected.

keyInsights: Submission criteria (formerly called validations) combine conditions based on user context, parameters, and static information into logical statements that gate action submission.
Conditions use two templates: 'Current User' (checking user identity, groups, or Multipass attributes) and 'Parameter' (checking action parameter values).
Logical operators (AND, OR, NOT) combine and nest conditions into sophisticated business logic statements.
Each action type has independent submission criteria, separate from the permissions governing whether a user can edit the action type itself.
Group membership checks support both direct and inherited membership, compared against static selections or parameter-provided group IDs.
Submission criteria are independent from action type edit permissions, meaning a user who can configure an action type is not necessarily allowed to submit it.

title: Interfaces - Overview - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: An abstract Ontology type that describes the shape and capabilities of object types, enabling polymorphism through shared properties and link type constraints.

extractedEntities.name: Interface Property
extractedEntities.description: A property defined on an interface, either locally (recommended) or via shared properties, that implementing object types must conform to.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type Constraint
extractedEntities.description: A constraint defined on an interface that specifies expected link relationships, which implementing object types must satisfy with concrete link types.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: A concrete Ontology type backed by datasets that can implement one or more interfaces to indicate conformance to their shape definitions.

extractedEntities.name: Shared Property
extractedEntities.description: A reusable property definition that can be used to define interface properties, though local interface properties are recommended.

extractedEntities.name: Interface Polymorphism
extractedEntities.description: A design pattern where workflows interact with object types through their interface rather than concrete type, enabling automatic compatibility with new implementing types.

extractedEntities.name: Interface Inheritance
extractedEntities.description: A pattern where interfaces extend other interfaces to create child interfaces that inherit parent properties and add more specific ones, supporting multiple inheritance.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Manager
extractedEntities.description: The Foundry tool where interfaces are defined, edited, and implemented by object types.

extractedEntities.name: Marketplace
extractedEntities.description: A Foundry feature where interfaces can be packaged and installed as reusable Ontology components.

extractedEntities.name: Interface Permissions
extractedEntities.description: Access control for interfaces managed through Ontology roles, governing who can view, edit, or use interface definitions.

extractedEntities.name: Facility Interface
extractedEntities.description: An example where a Facility interface with Name and Location properties is implemented by Airport, Manufacturing Plant, and Maintenance Hangar object types.

extractedEntities.name: Functions
extractedEntities.description: TypeScript v2 functions in Foundry that support interfaces for type-polymorphic business logic.

keyInsights: Interfaces are abstract Ontology types that cannot be instantiated directly; they must be instantiated as a specific implementing object type.
Object types can implement multiple interfaces for use in different workflows, and interfaces can extend multiple other interfaces supporting multiple inheritance.
Interface properties can be defined locally on the interface (recommended) or via shared properties.
Interfaces support link type constraints that specify expected relationships, though actions cannot reference interface link type constraints directly.
Interfaces are visually distinguished from object types in the platform with dashed lines around their icons.
When new object types implementing an interface are introduced, existing workflows using that interface become immediately compatible without refactoring.
Interfaces are currently fully supported in Ontology Manager, Marketplace, and TypeScript v2 Functions, with partial support in Actions and Object Set Service.
